executive_summary.narrative: This release is dominated by infrastructure misconfigurations: project-level IAM over-privilege, disabled Workload Identity, an open firewall rule, and several lower-severity hardening gaps.

top_risks.title: Project Owner role granted to a service account
top_risks.impact: Broadest possible GCP privilege granted at the project level.
top_risks.why_it_matters: Any workload using this service account inherits full project access.

top_risks.title: Workload Identity disabled
top_risks.impact: Pods inherit node-level credentials via the metadata server instead of scoped per-pod identity.
top_risks.why_it_matters: Removes the standard GKE mechanism for limiting blast radius of a compromised pod.
top_risks.recommended_action: Enable Workload Identity on the cluster and node pools.

priority_actions.title: Enable Workload Identity and scope IAM to least privilege together
priority_actions.rationale: These two findings compound each other; fixing both at once closes the path between them.

release_readiness.rationale: Critical IAM over-privilege combined with disabled Workload Identity is an unmitigated, high-confidence finding pair.
